enonce.texte:
📝 Exercise 1: Match the safety rules with their correct meanings.

Column A (Safety Rules)

  • 1. Wash your hands
  • 2. Dispose of trash properly
  • 3. Wear a helmet
  • 4. Look both ways
  • 5. Eat clean food

Column B (Meanings)

  • A. Check left and right before crossing
  • B. Use soap and water to clean your hands
  • C. Protect your head when riding a bike
  • D. Put rubbish in the bin
  • E. Only eat food that is safe and not spoiled
images: A collage of simple safety illustrations: a child washing hands, putting rubbish in a bin, wearing a helmet, looking both ways before crossing, eating healthy food

questions.texte: Match each safety rule in Column A with its meaning in Column B.

sousQuestions.texte: Wash your hands
sousQuestions.reponse: B. Use soap and water to clean your hands

sousQuestions.texte: Dispose of trash properly
sousQuestions.reponse: D. Put rubbish in the bin

sousQuestions.texte: Wear a helmet
sousQuestions.reponse: C. Protect your head when riding a bike

sousQuestions.texte: Look both ways
sousQuestions.reponse: A. Check left and right before crossing

sousQuestions.texte: Eat clean food
sousQuestions.reponse: E. Only eat food that is safe and not spoiled

enonce.texte:
🔍 Exercise 2: Read the text about safety needs and answer the questions.

"At home and at school, safety is very important. Clean water is necessary for drinking and washing. Safe food helps us stay healthy. A clean environment prevents diseases. We must always follow safety rules. At home, we should keep floors dry to avoid falling. At school, we should walk carefully in the corridors. We must also report any dangerous situations to our teachers."

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the text.

sousQuestions.texte: Why is clean water important? (Identify)
sousQuestions.reponse: Clean water is necessary for drinking and washing.

sousQuestions.texte: What does a clean environment prevent? (Identify)
sousQuestions.reponse: A clean environment prevents diseases.

sousQuestions.texte: List two safety rules mentioned in the text. (List)
sousQuestions.reponse: 1) Keep floors dry to avoid falling. 2) Walk carefully in the corridors.

sousQuestions.texte: What should we do when we see a dangerous situation? (Identify)
sousQuestions.reponse: We should report it to our teachers.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why safe food is important for our health. (Explain)
sousQuestions.reponse: Safe food is important because it helps us stay healthy. Unsafe food can cause food poisoning, stomach problems, and other diseases.

enonce.texte:
✍️ Exercise 3: Complete the sentences with the correct words from the box.

Vocabulary box: clean, safe, report, wash, walk, follow, avoid, keep

1. We must always _______ our hands before eating.

2. _______ food is important for good health.

3. We should _______ the safety rules at all times.

4. _______ any dangerous situation to your teacher.

5. We must _______ water to drink and to wash.

6. _______ carefully in the corridors at school.

7. _______ floors dry to avoid falling.

8. We should _______ places that are not safe.

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the vocabulary box.
questions.reponse: 1. wash, 2. Safe, 3. follow, 4. Report, 5. have/keep, 6. Walk, 7. Keep, 8. avoid

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 1: We must always _______ our hands before eating.
sousQuestions.reponse: wash

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 2: _______ food is important for good health.
sousQuestions.reponse: Safe

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 3: We should _______ the safety rules at all times.
sousQuestions.reponse: follow

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 4: _______ any dangerous situation to your teacher.
sousQuestions.reponse: Report

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 5: We must _______ water to drink and to wash.
sousQuestions.reponse: have/keep

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 6: _______ carefully in the corridors at school.
sousQuestions.reponse: Walk

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 7: _______ floors dry to avoid falling.
sousQuestions.reponse: Keep

sousQuestions.texte: Sentence 8: We should _______ places that are not safe.
sousQuestions.reponse: avoid

enonce.texte:
🎨 Exercise 4: Create safety rules for different places.

Instructions: Write three safety rules for each of the following places:

  • At home
  • At school
  • On the road

Use imperative sentences (commands) to give instructions.
images: Three different scenes: a home interior, a school corridor, and a road with traffic, each showing safe behaviors

questions.texte: Write three safety rules for each place using imperative sentences.
questions.reponse:
Example answers:

At home:
1. Keep floors dry to avoid falls.
2. Store chemicals out of children's reach.
3. Check food expiration dates before eating.

At school:
1. Walk calmly in the corridors.
2. Report any dangerous objects to your teacher.
3. Stay in the school compound during breaks.

On the road:
1. Look both ways before crossing the street.
2. Wear a helmet when riding a bicycle.
3. Always use pedestrian crossings.

sousQuestions.texte: Safety rules at home (3 rules)
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Keep floors dry to avoid falls. 2. Store chemicals out of children's reach. 3. Check food expiration dates before eating.

sousQuestions.texte: Safety rules at school (3 rules)
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Walk calmly in the corridors. 2. Report any dangerous objects to your teacher. 3. Stay in the school compound during breaks.

sousQuestions.texte: Safety rules on the road (3 rules)
sousQuestions.reponse: 1. Look both ways before crossing the street. 2. Wear a helmet when riding a bicycle. 3. Always use pedestrian crossings.

enonce.texte:
⚠️ Exercise 5: Read the safety scenarios and propose solutions.

Scenario A: At school, some students run in the corridors during break time. They sometimes bump into other students and cause accidents.

Scenario B: In the neighbourhood, there is a lot of garbage on the streets. Children play near the garbage and sometimes get sick.

Scenario C: Some students do not wear helmets when riding their bicycles to school. This is dangerous.
images: A school corridor with running students, a street with garbage, and a child on a bicycle without a helmet

questions.texte: Analyze each scenario and propose solutions.

sousQuestions.texte: Scenario A: Propose a solution to stop students from running in the corridors. (Propose)
sousQuestions.reponse: Solution: The school should put up signs that say "No running in the corridors". Teachers and prefects should remind students to walk. Students who run should be given a warning.

sousQuestions.texte: Scenario B: Propose a solution to solve the garbage problem. (Propose)
sousQuestions.reponse: Solution: The community should organize a clean-up campaign. They should place more garbage bins in the streets. The local authorities should collect garbage regularly. People should be educated about the dangers of littering.

sousQuestions.texte: Scenario C: Propose a solution to make students wear helmets. (Propose)
sousQuestions.reponse: Solution: The school should make it a rule that all students must wear helmets when riding bicycles. Parents should ensure their children have helmets. The school can organize a safety awareness campaign about the importance of wearing helmets.
